role_title: Principal Product Manager, Real-Time Payments Platform

level_signals: Owns vision, strategy, and roadmap for a shared infrastructure platform serving every line of business

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience, with significant time in payments, financial infrastructure, or money movement platforms.
must_haves.qualifier: with significant time in payments, financial infrastructure, or money movement platforms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep understanding of payment rails — you can explain the difference between FedNow, Fedwire, RTP, ACH, and card-based instant payments in your sleep. You understand message formats, settlement mechanics, cutoff times, and regulatory requirements for each.

Platform product management experience — you've built infrastructure or platform products that serve multiple internal or external customers, not just a single consumer surface. You think in APIs, contracts, and capabilities, not just screens.
Experience with payment orchestration — routing logic, fallback strategies, rail selection, transaction lifecycle management. Ideally you've worked with or built a payment hub or gateway.

Vendor and partner management chops — you've managed relationships with processors, networks, or infrastructure vendors. You know how to influence a partner's roadmap when you're not their biggest customer.
Comfort with compliance and risk — you understand AML/KYC/OFAC requirements for real-time payments, Reg E implications, and how to design for compliance without killing the user experience.
Strong analytical skills — you use data to identify problems, size opportunities, and make decisions. You can define and instrument metrics from scratch when the data doesn't exist yet.
Excellent communication — you can translate complex technical infrastructure into clear narratives for executive leadership, business partners, and operations teams.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at a bank, neobank, or bank-as-a-service provider (not just a fintech that rides on someone else's charter).
Familiarity with Galileo's platform (Payment Hub, Cyberbank Core) or similar core banking processors.
Experience building for both consumer and commercial (B2B) use cases on the same platform.
Background in real-time gross settlement (RTGS) systems, cross-border payments, or correspondent banking.
Technical depth — you can read an API spec, review an architecture diagram, or write a SQL query to investigate an issue. You don't need to code, but you're credible with engineers.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: SoFi's real-time payments platform — FedNow, Fedwire, instant debit (Visa Direct, Mastercard Send), RTP, and future real-time rails; shared infrastructure serving Consumer, Commercial Banking, Invest, Lending, and other business lines
scope.business_model: internal platform (B2B2C); 'AWS of Banking' model serving internal SoFi business lines and commercial clients
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for SoFi's real-time payments platform, including FedNow, Fedwire, instant debit (Visa Direct, Mastercard Send), and future real-time rails
Scale real-time capabilities to new business lines and onboard new use cases
Partner with Risk and Compliance and Operations to ensure real-time payments are secure, compliant, and resilient
Manage vendor and partner relationships including Galileo, the Federal Reserve, The Clearing House, and card network partners
Drive operational excellence — define SLAs, monitor platform health, reduce failure rates, and build operational tooling
Define and instrument metrics; build data infrastructure to track platform health

green_flags: Clear and well-scoped product surface with named rails (FedNow, Fedwire, RTP, ACH, Visa Direct, Mastercard Send)
Specific metrics articulated in the JD (transaction success rate, cost per transaction, fraud rates, complaints per rail)
Named vendors and partners provide concrete context for scope
Compelling platform-level ownership with cross-LOB impact ('AWS of Banking' framing)
EEO statement and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance compliance signals inclusive hiring
red_flags: Compensation range referenced ('The base pay range for this role is listed below') but not actually included in the JD — candidates cannot evaluate comp without applying
Location type is ambiguous — no city or remote policy explicitly stated in the JD body; only Hawaii/Alaska exclusion mentioned
No explicit statement about people management vs. IC expectations, though framing implies IC
